kept.text:
⚡⚡⚡近期觀察：中國市場傳 Model Y 有「大動作」？

👉 近期中國 Tesla 有消息指出：「這幾天訂 Model Y 不要急著提車，有大動作。」可以相信的原因是：去年同博主提前爆料 Model Y 外放電功能也真的推出；目前交付的 Model Y 也確實是小改款（16 吋螢幕、黑色車頂棚）。

但是預測項目目前沒有任何 Tesla 官方證實。

✍️ 把市場推測拆開來看會比較清楚。已被合理排除的項目包含 AI 4.5（馬斯克 Q1 財報明確說 AI 4 Plus 要 2027 年）、FSD 大優惠（中國 FSD 還在 WF1 測試）。

5 大核心預測則包含：
✅ 800V 高壓平台（Tesla 仍以 400V 為主）、
✅ 48V 低壓系統（Cybertruck 已採用）、
✅ 線控轉向 Steer-by-Wire（Cybertruck 獨家）、
✅ 後輪轉向（Cybertruck 獨家）、
✅ 4680 電池（美國部分 Y 已恢復使用 Gen 2）。

這 5 項技術 Cybertruck 都已驗證，但要複製到 Model Y 上目前沒有任何官方消息。

kept.text:
🚨“차 안 팔려도 괜찮다”…테슬라를 ‘AI 제국’으로 보는 투자자들

1️⃣ 주가 하락에도 6,500억 원 순매수 지속
테슬라 주가가 올해 약 17% 하락했음에도 국내 개인 투자자들은 한 달간 약 6,500억 원을 순매수하며 매수세를 이어갔습니다. 이는 단기 반등이 아닌 장기 성장 기대에 기반한 투자로 해석됩니다.

2️⃣ “전기차 기업 → AI·플랫폼 기업” 인식 전환
투자자들은 테슬라를 단순 자동차 제조사가 아니라 AI, 로보틱스, 우주 인프라를 결합한 플랫폼 기업으로 바라보고 있으며, 기업 가치 평가 기준 자체가 변화하고 있습니다.

3️⃣ 가격 경쟁 속에서도 수익성 방어
2026년 1분기 영업이익 9억 달러(+136% YoY), 매출 223억 달러로 시장 기대치를 상회하며, 전기차 가격 경쟁 심화 속에서도 수익성과 생존력을 동시에 입증했습니다.

4️⃣ 로보택시·AI·반도체에 대규모 투자 확대
250억 달러 이상의 자본지출을 통해 로보택시, AI, 자체 반도체 생산(테라팹)에 집중 투자하며 차량 판매 이후에도 수익을 창출하는 구조로 전환을 가속하고 있습니다.

5️⃣ 자체 칩 생산으로 ‘AI 인프라 기업’ 전환 시도

quoted_tweet.text:
Tesla is the most successful CON in the history of capital markets.

Not because the cars are bad.

But because the entire business is engineered to impress on first glance and collapse under scrutiny. And the culture around it has made facts completely IRRELEVANT.

I've never seen a company where the gap between what is promised and what is delivered is this wide, for this long, with this little accountability.

Tesla's Full Self-Driving system is marketed as autonomy. But it is not autonomy. It is a camera-only system running probabilistic inference. 

The car is making statistical guesses about what it sees, thousands of times per second, with no redundancy when those guesses are wrong. 

Probabilistic inference controlling a two-ton vehicle at highway speed with your family inside.

NHTSA has two open investigations covering 3.2 million Tesla vehicles. One was escalated to a formal Engineering Analysis in March after 9 crashes, including a fatality, where the system FAILED to detect sun glare, fog, and dust. The cameras went blind and the car kept driving.

In Austin, Tesla's robotaxi fleet has reported 15 crashes across roughly 800,000 miles. One crash every 57,000 miles. 

The average American driver has a police-reported crash every 500,000 miles. Tesla's robotaxis crash at roughly 4x the human rate, WITH a safety monitor sitting in the car whose only job is to prevent crashes.

Waymo operates over 2,500 fully driverless vehicles across multiple cities with no human backup and maintains a crash rate 85% below human drivers across 127 million autonomous miles. Tesla has ONE unsupervised vehicle in a tiny section of Austin.

But here's what really makes Tesla different from every overvalued company I've ever analyzed:

The facts do not matter to the people who own this stock.

Every missed deadline, every broken promise gets filtered through the same response: attack the messenger. 

Call them a short seller. Call them a hater. Anything to avoid looking at the actual numbers.

It's an online ecosystem that has made itself completely immune to facts. And Musk baked that dynamic into the culture from the beginning. 

Every time the fundamentals deteriorate, the faithful don't sell. They double down. 

When your shareholder base treats every dip as a buying opportunity regardless of the data, the stock becomes untethered from reality entirely.

That's literally a religion with a ticker symbol.

I highly suggest you read Edward Niedermeyer's book Ludicrous on this.

And now it even gets WORSE...

CapeFearAdvisors published a piece this week that should be required reading. 

Tesla's 2025 CEO Performance Award contains a change-of-control provision:

In the event of a change of control, ALL operational milestones are disregarded. 

No million robotaxis, Optimus robots, or $400 billion EBITDA. 
NONE of it. 

So if SpaceX acquires Tesla at $8.5 trillion, every tranche of Musk's 423 million share award vests immediately. A single acquisition at that price triggers the full vesting of both plans at once, with no way to claw them back.

The milestones everyone argues about are just a distraction. The mechanism is the change-of-control language buried in the SEC filing.

This is about engineering the largest personal wealth transfer in modern financial history and using the narrative machine to keep the price elevated long enough to execute it.

I've seen every bust of the last four decades.

But this one is different because the cult of personality is stronger than anything I've witnessed. The movement around this stock cannot be touched by facts, and that is what makes it so dangerous.

But the math always wins. ALWAYS.

It just takes longer when the con is this good.

kept.text:
Gonna keep talking about how good of a deal this is for a little while because I still can’t believe how hard Tesla cooked with the Standard Model 3. 

With gas prices sky high, the efficiency and cost to fuel this thing combine to make this vehicle the most compelling sedan on the market. On city roads in mild temperatures, after a weekend of driving around, this thing is pacing to beat the below EPA rated mileage. It’s a knife through butter. 

They compromised very little with the Standard 3 to get cost down. The seats are nice af. Half cloth half leather, but they feel just as premium to me as the leather seats. Quality feels equal. No screen in the back doesn’t matter. I don’t even remember I have that thing in my premium Y. LED cabin lights are cool but not necessary. The only single thing I don’t love is that you don’t have seat adjustment controls on the actual seat and have to do it from the screen. But after you’ve got your position set, even that doesn’t matter since Tesla saves your seat settings to your cloud profile. 

And you’re still going to feel fast AF in this thing because constant torque is like going from analog to digital. EV drivetrain is just superior. And this car runs FSD the same way the most expensive Tesla on the market runs FSD. The most insane consumer tech available today is fully accessible here. 

Getting a Camry or some similarly priced sedan is insane at this point. Tesla cooked hard with this one. Best value EV ever made.

kept.text:
The Warp wheels on the Model 3 Performance are genuinely one of the best looking factory wheels Tesla has ever made.
⠀
20 inch forged. Staggered fitment. Aggressive spokes. They look fantastic.
⠀
The only catch?
⠀
They are an absolute mission to clean.
⠀
Every spoke. Every gap. Dirt absolutely cakes into the wheel well and behind the aero inserts. A couple of minutes per wheel at minimum.
⠀
And then there's the aero inserts.
⠀
Been umming and ahhing about pulling them since day one. The problem isn't dirt underneath - it's that pulling them out leaves tiny scratches on the wheel behind the insert. 33,000km in now.
⠀
Is it worth exposing that and getting an easier clean & looking better?
⠀
Or do we leave them in and keep up the cleaning?

🚨노르웨이 4월 테슬라 판매 — 점유율 하락 속 BEV 시장은 역대 최고

1️⃣ 판매량 및 점유율
4월 테슬라 판매 281대, 전체 시장 점유율 2.5%
BEV 침투율은 98.6%로 역대 최고 기록, 이 중 테슬라 비중은 2.6%

2️⃣ 모델 믹스
Model Y 81%, Model 3 19%
👉 여전히 Model Y 중심 구조 유지

3️⃣ 전년 대비 및 단기 흐름
전년 동월 대비 -71% 감소
하지만 직전 분기 시작점(1월) 대비 +239% 증가
👉 분기 초 대비 회복세는 뚜렷

4️⃣ 최근 3개월 추세
최근 3개월 누적 기준 -36.2% (11월~1월 대비)
👉 단기적으로는 둔화 흐름 지속

5️⃣ 연간 누적(YTD) 흐름
전년 동기 대비 +61% 증가
현재 누적은 작년 전체의 23% 수준 (2.7/12 진행)
👉 연간 기준으로는 여전히 성장 궤도 유지

결론
노르웨이는 BEV 전환이 사실상 완료된 시장(98.6%)으로,
테슬라는 점유율 변동성이 커졌지만
연간 기준 성장세는 유지되는 구조.

kept.text:
I was telling someone about today's trip and how the Tesla Trip planner shows me getting to my destination with 10% remaining. This would be fine but the nearest supercharger is 30 miles away. It didn't account for having to get to another charger after arriving. There are slow AC chargers, but nothing that I have time to wait for.
Then the conversation turned to - "you never had to do any of this mental gymnastics to plan for charging with your F-150, there's always a gas station at your destination." Which is true, I had never planned a trip with any of my other vehicles around whether or not there would be somewhere to get fuel.

kept.text:
Grok 4 Heavy is fast because it’s parallel.

When you ask it a hard question, it spawns multiple agents at the same time. Each one tackles the problem independently. 

Heavy then synthesizes their outputs into a single answer.

Most chatbots are single-threaded. One model, one pass, one answer.

Grok 4 Heavy is multi-threaded. The harder the question, the more parallelism kicks in. The architecture is the speed.

TL;DR 🚨 Tesla's zero marginal cost fastener fix in a nutshell 🛠️

💥 Traditional interior trim fasteners frequently ruin themselves when pushed into a car's metal frame at a crooked angle, causing a sharp impact that prematurely locks the mechanism and wastes millions in factory downtime.

🛡️ To solve this, Tesla engineered a tiny deflection shield by adding a flared "snap" to the very bottom of the fastener's inner pin, absorbing the impact and protecting the vulnerable outer locking sleeve.

🧲 This sloped, funnel-like shield actively catches the raw metal edge and forces the entire fastener to slide straight into perfect alignment.

☂️ Because the shield is wider than the outer sleeve, it is designed to be highly flexible, squeezing inward to slide through the narrow opening during factory assembly before popping back out to its full protective width.

📳 Once properly seated, a built-in elastic tab delivers a distinct physical snap, giving human hands and automated robot grippers instant tactile confirmation of a successful fit.

⚙️ Instead of relying on rigid, pre-set notches to pull the panels together, the fastener uses a continuous helical spiral thread, allowing for infinite tension adjustments to close panel gaps flawlessly.

🧩 The top of the pin uses a modular two-piece head that securely sandwiches the interior trim while spreading out the clamping pressure to prevent any cosmetic damage.

📐 The exact angles of the deflection shield can be easily tuned in the injection mold, creating customized hardware variations for heavy acoustic panels or lightweight plastics.

🤖 By eliminating the assembly line failure rate without adding a single cent to the bill of materials, this self-guiding hardware clears the path for Optimus robots to build fully automated vehicle interiors without jamming or breaking parts.

kept.text:
🔥🚀 Tesla Spring Update 2026.14.3 is now rolling out to the 9th batch of vehicles (reaching ~64% of the fleet)!

Only bug fixes are expected compared to 2026.14.1 and .2.
Notice anything new? Feel free to share!

2026.14.1 changes:
✅ Self-Driving App [AI4]
With the new Self-Driving app, subscribe with a single tap, learn how to activate and use the feature, and view ongoing stats.

✅ "Hey Grok"
Launch Grok by saying "Hey Grok." Go to Grok > Settings to enable. You can also set location based reminders, like "remind me to pick up milk when I'm near home." Say "goodbye" to dismiss.

✅ Pet Mode
Choose and name your pet by going to Controls > Display > Customize Pet Mode.

✅ Visual Updates [New Model 3/Y]
The center display now features higher quality images of your Tesla, and an enhanced park scene environment.

✅ Trips
Trips are now in the Energy app, where you can create and manage multiple trips to track energy usage across different drives, routes, or conditions. Go to App Launcher > Energy > Trips. For quick access to the Trips Card, swipe left on the Media Card.

✅ Sketchpad
Unleash your inner artist with the enhanced Sketchpad, now supporting stickers and emojis. Save your sketches to access them in the Mobile App and share.

✅ Custom Wraps and License Plates [S/X]
Model S & X owners can now personalize their Tesla avatar with window tints, custom wraps & license plates.

✅ Blind Spot Warning Accent Lights
Accent lights now turn red when an object is in your blind spot and your turn signal is engaged, or when an approaching object is detected while parked. To disable, go to Controls > Lights > Blind Spot Warning Accent Lights.

✅ Immersive Sound Upgrade [New Model 3 & Y w/ premium audio]
Premium Immersive Sound uses advanced sound extraction to place the listener in front of a detailed soundstage within an Immersive space. This enhancement works with all streaming sources.

✅ Music App Queuing
Swipe right on tracks in Apple Music or Spotify to add them to the queue. In Apple Music, tap and hold on a track to add or remove from Favorites. In Spotify, swipe left to add or remove from Liked Songs.

✅ Automatic Software Updates
Your Tesla can now automatically install downloaded software updates overnight. When enabled, updates will install while your Tesla is parked and not in use, ensuring you always have the latest features and improvements.
To enable, go to Controls > Software > Automatically Install Updates.

✅ Dashcam Viewer Updates
Recent Dashcam footage can now extend up to 24 hours, and you can save any clip for permanent storage on your Tesla or on your phone by going to App Launcher > Dashcam Viewer.
Clips downloaded to your phone now include details such as speed, steering wheel angle, and Self-Driving state.

✅ Weather Maps Improvements
Weather maps now show snow and rain with improved colors that make it easier to distinguish between precipitation types. You can also view the past hour of weather data to see how conditions have been changing along your route.

✅ Supercharger Pricing Filter
You can now choose to route only to Superchargers with Tesla pricing. When enabled, Trip Planner will avoid locations where Tesla does not set charging prices or accept Free Supercharging credits.

✅ Keyboard
Your selected keyboard language will also select the language used for dictation. Tap the international keyboard button to change keyboard and dictation language, without changing voice command language. Tap the mic icon in the keyboard to dictate.

✅ Rear Display
Passengers in rear seats can now view and interact with maps on the rear display while your Tesla navigates a route. Tap the maps icon from the rear display to follow along.

✅ Security Improvements

✅ Larger icons on the bottom bar [undocumented]

✅ Animated badge in software menu [undocumented]

✅ Audio source icon next to the volume slider [undocumented]

quoted_tweet.text:
Tesla-Erstkontakt Mein Fazit nach dem ersten Tag 🚗⚡
Endlich ist es soweit. Meine Freundin hat ihren Tesla abgeholt und ich bin ehrlich gesagt richtig begeistert. Hätte ich nie gedacht, dass mich das so packt. Liebe meinen Verbrenner heiss und innig. 
Gestern durfte ich das erste Mal richtig fahren und ein paar Dinge testen.
Die Beschleunigung mit rund 500 PS ist einfach nur brutal. Man grinst automatisch wie ein Retard. 
Die Bedienung über den großen Bildschirm ist am Anfang ungewohnt und man sucht noch ein paar Dinge. Aber nach 30 Minuten hat man den Dreh raus und will eigentlich nie wieder zu klassischen Knöpfen zurück. Sehr clean und wahrscheinlich eh die Zukunft. 
Die reale Reichweite hat mich positiv überrascht. Bei normaler Fahrweise und etwas Autobahn kommt man echt weit. Für den Alltag mehr als ausreichend.
Und dann das große Thema Verarbeitung. Ja, man hört und liest immer wieder, dass Tesla da nicht auf Premium-Niveau wie Mercedes oder BMW sei. Türen, Spaltmaße, Materialien 
,ich bin gespannt, wie lange alles top bleibt. Bisher sieht alles solide aus, aber ich halte die Augen offen, wann die ersten Tesla-Teile fliegen 😄
Fazit nach Tag 1:
Tesla ist kein klassisches Auto ,es ist ein Tech-Produkt auf Rädern. Und genau das macht es so spannend. Die Fahrfreude ist auf einem ganz anderen Level.
Besonders interessiert mich, wie sich die Verarbeitung langfristig hält und wie der Service bei Tesla so abläuft und natürlich auch wie sich die Unterhaltskosten entwickeln. Jetzt kommt sicher ne Hatewelle auf mich zu, weil Tesla ist doof 🤪 I don’t care at all.

kept.text:
Thoughts on the trade today $TSLA
Rolled my 420 June short calls out to Jan 2027 500s.

Bought them back at $11.28 and sold the new ones for $29.40. Nice credit.

That’s roughly 8% premium for the next 8 months — right in my sweet spot of 1-2% per month.

I had three choices:
1.  Do nothing (capping upside on those lots)
2.  Take them off completely
3.  Roll them

I went with #3.

I prefer keeping shorter-term calls for quick in-and-out intraday trades. These June ones were only on since last Friday. I’ve historically made the most selling LEAPs over $400 and managing the bigger swings.

If we dump, I can roll these down and in in time more easily than trying to add new calls in a panic. If we gap up big, I can roll down in strike and in time to capture the targeted gal fill, then roll back up later. 

Managing good problems.

I currently have 40 calls sold. I’m debating adding 10-20 more. 

Overall I’m just trying to maximize premium on flat/pullback periods while staying ready for the rips. Managing things upward as the stock climbs is easy and exactly where I want the account — pushing ATHs and beating the indexes.

The rips I’ll deal with happily when they come.
